← All Digests Tuesday, March 10, 2026

Mega Raises $11.5M for AI Marketing Agents, OpenAI Pulls Back on Agentic Commerce, AI Threatens $38B Retail Media Market

Today's top stories in AI + Product Marketing

1

Mega Raises $11.5M to Replace Marketing Agencies with AI Agents

Mega just closed a Series A led by Goodwater Capital with a16z and SignalFire participating. The startup's pitch: replace your entire marketing agency with a network of AI agents that execute SEO, paid ads, GEO optimization, and website management end-to-end. Their hybrid model runs 55% fully automated, 35% AI-assisted, and 10% human. If this scales, the traditional agency model is in serious trouble.

Read on Efficiently Connected →
2

OpenAI Scales Back "Agentic Commerce" — Instant Checkout Removed

Big reality check: OpenAI just nixed its Instant Checkout feature that let users buy directly in ChatGPT. Shoppers will now be redirected back to merchant websites or apps. Per Forrester, only 23% of Gen X and 35% of Gen Z have used ChatGPT for product discovery — adoption is growing but far from mainstream. The "death of retail websites" narrative just got a lot more nuanced.

Read on Forrester →
3

AI Could Disrupt Retail Media's $38 Billion Search Ad Market

Digiday digs into how AI chatbots could rattle retail media networks. Gartner predicts traditional search volume will drop 25% this year due to AI chatbots. With retail media search spend hitting $38B in 2025, that's a lot of dollars at risk. Amazon's Rufus and Walmart's Sparky are already in the game — but marketers say they're not moving budgets yet. The measurement story isn't there.

Read on Digiday →
4

LLMs Are Now the "Front Door" for Product Discovery

Traditional SEO is giving way to "Generative Engine Optimization" (GEO) as LLMs become the primary discovery channel. Adobe's new LLM Optimizer claims 200%+ lift in LLM visibility and 41% increase in AI-referred traffic. McKinsey and IDC are calling 2025-2026 the inflection point. Forecasts suggest companies may spend 5× more on LLM optimization than traditional SEO by decade's end.

Read on Tekedia →
5

Teradata Launches Enterprise AI Agents for Multimodal Data

Teradata just dropped major upgrades to its Enterprise Vector Store — enabling AI agents to autonomously process text, images, and audio at enterprise scale. With LangChain integration and support for 8K embedding dimensions, they're targeting the agentic AI infrastructure layer. Nearly 80% of companies are already deploying AI agents, with most projecting 100%+ ROI from agentic initiatives.

Read on MarTech Series →

💡 My Take

Read this one: The Forrester piece on OpenAI scaling back agentic commerce. It's a great reality check amid the hype. Only 23-35% of consumers are even using AI for product discovery — and the "death of retail websites" narrative just took a hit. The opportunity is real, but the timeline is longer than the hype suggests. Smart PMMs are preparing for the transition without betting the farm.
